div.head: Memorandum by the Assistant Secretary of State (Sayre)
div.p: In regard to the Finnish treaty the only thing that remains to be done is the comparing of the Finnish and English texts by the Department. Arrangements are now being made to obtain the services of a Finnish translator to do this work, which may take several days.

The Finnish Minister has already gone over the two texts and found a few errors, which have been corrected. He has left with the Department his full power, and consequently he is ready to sign the treaty as soon as we notify him that we are ready.
